weekly_reports.aiSummary: Weekly road audit: 153 potholes tracked across Bengaluru, with 63 repaired (41% resolution rate — moderate). 90 remain unresolved, including 40 critical-severity cases. Electronic City is the worst-performing locality this period, while Indiranagar leads in repairs. 68 SLA breaches recorded, and 16 potholes have reoccurred after previous repairs. Priority: deploy crews to critical zones and audit repair quality.

aiInsights.summary: This week: 153 potholes reported, 63 fixed (41% resolution — moderate). 90 remain pending with 40 critical-severity cases. Electronic City requires immediate attention while Indiranagar leads in repairs. Average fix time: 0.1 days.
issues: 40 critical-severity potholes (26% of total) pose immediate safety risks and require priority dispatch.
70% of all reports are high or critical severity — indicating widespread road deterioration rather than isolated incidents.
16 reoccurring potholes detected — previous repairs have failed, suggesting quality issues with patching materials or contractors.
patterns: Repair rate (63/week) is outpaced by new reports — the backlog of 90 pending potholes is likely growing.
16 reoccurrences indicate a repair quality pattern — same locations failing within weeks of patching.
Electronic City continues to be the highest-concentration area. Indiranagar ward shows the most responsive repair crews.
recommendations: Deploy emergency repair crews to Electronic City — 40 critical potholes need attention within 6-hour SLA windows.
Audit repair quality for 16 reoccurring sites. Consider switching to hot-mix asphalt or full-depth patching for repeat failures.
Replicate Indiranagar ward's repair workflow across underperforming zones to standardise response times.
